rulings.Case Title: MCGRATH ENTERPRISES LLC VS. NATIVE GROUP CORPORATION DBA BLACKTHORN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 22, 2024 line 1. PLAINTIFF MCGRATH ENTERPRISES LLC'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT is GRANTED. Defendants are in violation of the settlement agreement, which provides that every provision of the agreement is material and time is of the essence. Under the terms of the agreement, if the defendant fails to perform any duty or obligation plaintiff is entitled to a judgment for possession, damages, costs and attorney's fees with credit for any payments made by defendant pursuant to paragraph 1(b) of the agreement. The Court notes that defendants failed to comply with SFLR 2.7B. Failure to comply in the future will result in opposition not being considered. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: AVALONBAY COMMUNITIES, INC. VS. MARIA GONZALEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 22, 2024 line 3. HEARING ON ANDREA SANCHEZ'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION Hearing Required. Claimant, Andrea Sanchez, to present any evidence she wishes the Court to consider at the hearing. If claimant intends to appear remotely, hard copies of the evidence are to be delivered to the Court by no later than 9:30 a.m. on May 22, 2024 and served on the Plaintiff's counsel via e-mail (alternatively, a second set of the hard copies to be brought to the Court for Plaintiff's counsel). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
